titleStmt.title: Letter from James Davidson to Clara Davidson, 18 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Davidson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from James Davidson to his mother, Clara Mary (b.1852). James writes that he is keeping well. He warns that she may not hear from him for some time as they are 'on the eve of big events.' The Somme bombardment would begin six days later on 24 June. This letter is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
18/6/16 my dearest mother Just a very few
lines to tell you that
I am well. I don't
know whether this
will reach you as
postal arrangements
are upset. we are
on the eve of big events
so you must not be
alarmed if you don't
have news for perhaps
a week or even more.
fondest love to all your loving son James
On Active Service
letter 8th Wed 22nd
June/16 Mrs. S.C.Davidson Seacourt Bangor Co Down Ireland JDavidson
